pattern: Send everyone to the cheaper city first, then sorting by how much extra each switch to the other city costs reveals the cheapest n people to relocate.
hints: Imagine flying all 2n people to city A. Now exactly n of them must instead go to city B.
Switching person i from A to B changes the cost by bCost[i] - aCost[i]. You want the n switches that add the least (or subtract the most).
Sort the people by that difference and switch the n cheapest-to-switch; sum the resulting assignment costs.

edge_inputs.description: four people, two to each city
minimum size, one to each city
all prefer city A but only half can go
equal costs make every split optimal

stress.description: random even-length cost matrix kept small so the exponential oracle stays fast
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    pairs = max(1, min(size, 7))
    costs = [[rng.randint(1, 1000), rng.randint(1, 1000)] for _ in range(2 * pairs)]
    return [costs]
stress.note: 2*pairs people; combinations oracle stays under a second
